summary: Large decompilation sweep matching nearly all remaining 8-byte functions across MetroTRK, Dolphin SDK, sysdolphin baselib, melee core, fighter, item, stage, game-mode, and config areas. The PR body notes one explicit exception: `GetR2_80322F20`, deferred to #1181. The diff shows many tiny functions converted from `/// #symbol` placeholders into exact C stubs or trivial accessors/returners, plus broad header/prototype additions and split/symbol table adjustments needed to keep section boundaries and local sdata2 symbols aligned.
what_changed: Matched many 8-byte functions by replacing placeholder markers with tiny C implementations such as constant returns, false-return stubs, empty functions, simple setters, and global/static variable accessors.

Added or expanded headers with address-annotated prototypes using the project convention `/* address */ return_type symbol(args);`, often with `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and `<placeholder.h>` where exact types were still unknown.
Added initial source bodies for previously nearly-empty MetroTRK files including `mpc_7xx_603e.c`, `msgbuf.c`, `msghndlr.c`, and `targimpl.c`.

Converted specific prototypes from unknown placeholder signatures to exact small-function signatures, for example `TRKTerminateSerialHandler(void)`, `Hu_IsStub(void)`, `TRKInitializeMutex(void)`, `TRKAcquireMutex(void)`, and `TRKReleaseMutex(void)` returning `bool`.

Introduced static variables where required for accessor matches, including `target_cpu_minor_type = 84`, `__AXCommandListCycles`, `__AXUserFrameCallback`, `__AXNumVoices`, `gIsChannelinfoDirty`, and `gLastError` in shown excerpts.
Implemented PowerPC special-register helpers in `targimpl.c` with `MWERKS_GEKKO` inline assembly for `mfmsr` and `mtmsr`, while retaining `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks outside that target.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/splits.txt` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, including reducing some `.sdata2` split ranges and renumbering local float symbols, showing that matching tiny functions required data/symbol table cleanup too.
Applied style cleanup in Dolphin AX headers, notably pointer star placement such as `AXVPB*` and `void*`, while preserving SDK-style names.
smart_moves: Attacked a broad class of very small functions as a batch, which is efficient because many 8-byte functions share simple codegen patterns: return constant, return global, assign field, or empty/false stub.
Kept unresolved larger functions as `/// #symbol` placeholders while landing the trivial exact matches around them, allowing progress without overclaiming incomplete decompilation.
Used exact bool-returning signatures for known false-return stubs instead of leaving them as generic `UNK_RET`, improving type information and future callsite cleanup.
Added headers alongside source changes so the new matched functions were reusable by later decomp work and not isolated local implementations.
Preserved address comments in prototypes, making the sweep searchable and auditable against the original binary layout.
Handled architecture-specific inline assembly with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, avoiding non-target build breakage while still matching the intended GameCube compiler path.
Explicitly documented the one exception, `GetR2_80322F20`, preventing future agents from assuming the PR truly covered every 8-byte function.
decomp_lessons: For 8-byte function sweeps, first search for trivial placeholder functions that can be expressed as `return false;`, `return constant;`, empty function bodies, simple static/global accessors, or one-field stores.
Do not require perfect surrounding decompilation before landing tiny matched functions; it is acceptable to leave neighboring functions as `/// #...` placeholders when evidence is insufficient.
When matching tiny functions, check `.sdata2` and local symbol numbering: even a trivial C body can affect local constants and split boundaries.
Small function matching often depends more on correct prototypes and return types than on complex C logic.
A broad stub sweep should update headers in parallel, especially for library and SDK areas, so later files can include the right declarations rather than duplicating unknown signatures.
Use target-guarded inline assembly for CPU-register helpers; the decomp path can match while non-Gekko environments still have a defined fallback.
Explicitly track exceptions in the PR text or notes so future searches know what remains unmatched.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK functions keep official-looking `TRK...` names such as `TRKSetBufferUsed`, `TRKTerminateSerialHandler`, `TRKTargetCPUMinorType`, and `TRKInitializeMutex`.
Internal MetroTRK helper names use double-underscore style when matching low-level helpers, e.g. `__TRK_get_MSR` and `__TRK_set_MSR`.
Dolphin AX internals use double-underscore SDK-style names such as `__AXGetCommandListCycles`, `__AXGetNumVoices`, `__AXOutInit`, and `__AXSetPBDefault`.
Unknown functions retain address-derived placeholder names when no semantic name is known, for example `MTRK_MsgHndlr_8032785C` and `AXVPB_8035AE20`.
Header include guards in newly created MetroTRK headers use `GALE01_` plus an address-like suffix, for example `GALE01_326C94`, `GALE01_327740`, and `GALE01_32904C`.
Project prototypes frequently carry address comments in the form `/* 32904C */ int __TRK_get_MSR(void);`.
Pointer style in the touched AX external header was normalized to attach the star to the type, e.g. `AXVPB* p`, `void* buffer`, and `u32* p`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use minimal exact C for tiny functions: `return false;`, `return target_cpu_minor_type;`, `return __AXCommandListCycles;`, `return __AXNumVoices;`, and assignments to static callback/global state.
For simple struct/opaque field writes where the struct is not typed, `M2C_FIELD(arg0, int*, 4) = arg1;` can be used as an interim matching tactic, as shown in `TRKSetBufferUsed`.
For PowerPC MSR helpers, use Metrowerks inline assembly under `MWERKS_GEKKO`: `asm { mfmsr r3 }` for reading and `asm { mtmsr r3 }` for writing.
Keep nonmatching or larger functions as `/// #FunctionName` while adding matched 8-byte functions around them; this avoids destabilizing larger unmatched regions.
Introduce static file-scope storage when the binary function is just an accessor or setter for internal state.
Pair source implementation changes with exact prototypes to ensure the generated code and call ABI match.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only textual guidance in the PR body was: all 8-byte functions were matched except `GetR2_80322F20`, with a reference to #1181.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining references to `GetR2_80322F20` and PR #1181 to understand why it was excluded from the 8-byte sweep.
Search for `return false;` stubs added in this PR and verify whether any can now receive better semantic names or types.
Search for `M2C_FIELD` usages introduced or touched by this PR to replace opaque field access with real structs when known.
Review address-commented `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes added in MetroTRK, AX, MCC, item, stage, and game-mode files for future type refinement.
Check `.sdata2` split changes around `itpippi.c` and `itmato.c` when touching adjacent local constants or symbols.
Search for `/// #` placeholders adjacent to newly matched 8-byte functions to identify good next decompilation targets.
